diagrams.title: Basic Pigeonhole Principle Visualization
diagrams.description: Shows 6 pigeons (items) trying to fit into 5 holes (containers). One hole must contain 2 pigeons.
elements: Arrow showing overflow to first hole
Label: n=6 > m=5, so at least one hole has 2+ items
diagrams.purpose: Understanding the fundamental principle through visual representation

diagrams.title: Generalized Pigeonhole Principle
diagrams.description: 50 students distributed across 12 months showing guaranteed clustering
elements: Shading showing minimum 5 in one month: ⌈50/12⌉ = 5
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing the ceiling function result and distribution patterns

diagrams.title: Birthday Paradox Pair Counting
diagrams.description: Visual representation of why 23 people create high collision probability
elements: 253 connecting lines showing all possible pairs
diagrams.purpose: Building intuition about counterintuitive probability result

diagrams.title: Ramsey Number R(3,3) = 6
diagrams.description: Complete graph K6 with edges colored red/blue showing guaranteed monochromatic triangle
elements: Each edge colored red or blue randomly
Highlighting: any 2-coloring yields red or blue triangle
diagrams.purpose: Understanding extension of pigeonhole principle to graph coloring
